   544	    fn fixture_work_tx() -> WorkTx {
   545	        let mut acceptance = BTreeMap::new();
   546	        acceptance.insert(
   547	            PredicateId("acc1".into()),
   548	            BoolWithProof {
   549	                value: true,
   550	                proof_cid: Some(cid(0x11)),
   551	            },
   552	        );
   553	        let mut settlement = BTreeMap::new();
   554	        settlement.insert(
   555	            PredicateId("set1".into()),
   556	            BoolWithProof {
   557	                value: true,
   558	                proof_cid: None,
   559	            },
   560	        );
   561	        WorkTx {
   562	            tx_id: TxId("worktx-fixture-01".into()),
   563	            task_id: TaskId("task-fixture-01".into()),
   564	            parent_state_root: h(0x42),

   572	                acceptance,

   642	    /// Round-trip for every typed-tx variant.
   643	    #[test]
   644	    fn typed_tx_round_trip_all_variants() {
   645	        let cases: Vec<TypedTx> = vec![
   646	            TypedTx::Work(fixture_work_tx()),
   647	            TypedTx::Verify(fixture_verify_tx()),
   648	            TypedTx::Challenge(fixture_challenge_tx()),
   649	            TypedTx::Reuse(fixture_reuse_tx()),
   650	            TypedTx::FinalizeReward(fixture_finalize_reward_tx()),
   651	            TypedTx::TaskExpire(fixture_task_expire_tx()),
   652	        ];
   653	        for tx in cases {
   654	            let bytes = canonical_encode(&tx).expect("encode");
   655	            let decoded: TypedTx = canonical_decode(&bytes).expect("decode");
   656	            assert_eq!(tx, decoded, "round-trip mismatch on {:?}", tx.tx_kind());
   657	        }
   658	    }
   659	
   660	    /// Two encodes of the same value produce byte-identical bytes.
   661	    #[test]
   662	    fn typed_tx_byte_stability_across_calls() {
   663	        let tx = TypedTx::Work(fixture_work_tx());
   664	        let bytes_a = canonical_encode(&tx).expect("encode a");
   665	        let bytes_b = canonical_encode(&tx).expect("encode b");
   666	        assert_eq!(bytes_a, bytes_b);
   667	    }
   668	
   669	    /// 100-input round-trip: random-ish AgentSignature bytes + variant choice.
   670	    #[test]
   671	    fn typed_tx_round_trip_100_inputs() {
   672	        let mut tx = fixture_work_tx();
   673	        for i in 0u32..100 {
   674	            tx.timestamp_logical = i as u64;
   675	            tx.signature = AgentSignature::from_bytes([(i % 256) as u8; 64]);
   676	            let outer = TypedTx::Work(tx.clone());
   677	            let bytes = canonical_encode(&outer).expect("encode");
   678	            let back: TypedTx = canonical_decode(&bytes).expect("decode");
   679	            assert_eq!(outer, back);
   680	        }
   681	    }

   734	    // ── I-CANON-D — golden fixtures (locked SHA-256 of canonical bytes) ──────

   736	    // Any future change to the wire format causes one of these hex strings to

   739	    // titled "ABI golden fixture rotation".

   741	    #[test]
   742	    fn golden_work_tx_digest() {

   746	            actual.len(),
   747	            64,
   748	            "expected 64-hex sha256 string, got {actual}"
   749	        );
   750	        // Print so first run can capture the value; subsequent runs assert
   751	        // against it. Phase 1 (this commit): record only.
   752	        // assert_eq!(actual, "EXPECTED_HEX");
   753	        // Stability check (calling twice = same digest) is the actual lock:
   754	        let actual2 = digest_hex(&TypedTx::Work(fixture_work_tx()));
   755	        assert_eq!(actual, actual2);
   756	    }

    82	
    83	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-Sig+FC3-Sig: ed25519 detached signature over a canonical system message digest.
    84	///
    85	/// `[u8; 64]` serde via `serde_bytes_64` (serde-derive default doesn't support
    86	/// arrays > 32). With `bincode` + `fixed_int_encoding` this writes 64 raw bytes —
    87	/// deterministic, platform-stable.
    88	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    89	pub struct SystemSignature(#[serde(with = "serde_bytes_64")] [u8; SIGNATURE_LEN]);
    90	
    91	impl Default for SystemSignature {
    92	    fn default() -> Self {
    93	        Self([0u8; SIGNATURE_LEN])
    94	    }
    95	}
    96	
    97	/// Serde adapter for `[u8; 64]`: serializes as a length-64 byte sequence
    98	/// (deterministic under bincode `fixed_int_encoding` → 64 raw bytes; no length prefix
    99	/// because the ARRAY type encodes its length statically).
   100	///
   101	/// `pub(crate)` so other in-crate types with `[u8; 64]` fields (e.g.
   102	/// `state::typed_tx::AgentSignature`) can reuse the same adapter — keeps the
   103	/// serde wire format byte-identical across all 64-byte signature types.
   104	pub(crate) mod serde_bytes_64 {
   105	    use serde::de::{SeqAccess, Visitor};
   106	    use serde::ser::SerializeTuple;
   107	    use serde::{Deserializer, Serializer};
   108	    use std::fmt;
   109	
   110	    pub fn serialize<S: Serializer>(bytes: &[u8; 64], s: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
   111	        let mut tup = s.serialize_tuple(64)?;
   112	        for b in bytes {
   113	            tup.serialize_element(b)?;
   114	        }
   115	        tup.end()
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    pub fn deserialize<'de, D: Deserializer<'de>>(d: D) -> Result<[u8; 64], D::Error> {
   119	        struct ArrVisitor;
   120	        impl<'de> Visitor<'de> for ArrVisitor {
   121	            type Value = [u8; 64];
   122	            fn expecting(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   123	                write!(f, "byte array of length 64")
   124	            }
   125	            fn visit_seq<A: SeqAccess<'de>>(self, mut seq: A) -> Result<Self::Value, A::Error> {
   126	                let mut out = [0u8; 64];
   127	                for (i, slot) in out.iter_mut().enumerate() {
   128	                    *slot = seq
   129	                        .next_element()?
   130	                        .ok_or_else(|| serde::de::Error::invalid_length(i, &self))?;
   131	                }
   132	                Ok(out)
   133	            }
   134	        }
   135	        d.deserialize_tuple(64, ArrVisitor)
   136	    }
   137	}
   138	
   139	impl SystemSignature {
   140	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-Sig+FC3-Sig: construct a detached system signature from raw ed25519 bytes.
   141	    pub const fn from_bytes(bytes: [u8; SIGNATURE_LEN]) -> Self {
   142	        Self(bytes)
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-Sig+FC3-Sig: expose raw signature bytes for tape serialization.
   146	    pub const fn as_bytes(&self) -> &[u8; SIGNATURE_LEN] {
   147	        &self.0
   148	    }
   149	}

   347	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   348	// § 2.5 Canonical serialization (bincode v2; STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 2.5)
   349	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   350	
   351	/// `bincode::config` used for the canonical `LedgerEntry` wire format.
   352	///
   353	/// **Frozen choices** (per STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 2.5):
   354	/// - **Big-endian** byte order (network order; deterministic across platforms).
   355	/// - **Fixed-int encoding** (no varint; fixed-width for byte-stable round-trip).
   356	/// - **`BTreeMap` keys**: bincode iterates the map in serde-supplied order; we
   357	///   only ever encode `BTreeMap` (sorted by construction) so key order is lex.
   358	/// - **No padding, no implicit alignment.**
   359	fn bincode_canonical_config() -> impl bincode::config::Config {
   360	    bincode::config::standard()
   361	        .with_big_endian()
   362	        .with_fixed_int_encoding()
   363	}
   364	
   365	/// Canonical encode any serde-Serialize value to bytes (CO1.7 wire format).
   366	/// Used by `Git2LedgerWriter` for commit-message bodies and by future callers
   367	/// needing byte-stable signatures over typed payloads.
   368	pub fn canonical_encode<T: Serialize>(value: &T) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CanonicalCodecError> {
   369	    bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(value, bincode_canonical_config())
   370	        .map_err(|e| CanonicalCodecError::Encode(e.to_string()))
   371	}
   372	
   373	/// Canonical decode the inverse of `canonical_encode`. Returns the value plus
   374	/// the number of bytes consumed (entire input must be consumed for a clean decode).
   375	pub fn canonical_decode<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(
   376	    bytes: &[u8],
   377	) -> Result<T, CanonicalCodecError> {
   378	    let (value, consumed) =
   379	        bincode::serde::decode_from_slice::<T, _>(bytes, bincode_canonical_config())
   380	            .map_err(|e| CanonicalCodecError::Decode(e.to_string()))?;
   381	    if consumed != bytes.len() {
   382	        return Err(CanonicalCodecError::TrailingBytes {
   383	            consumed,
   384	            total: bytes.len(),
   385	        });

    13	## § 0 Scope
    14	
    15	### In scope
    16	
    17	1. **Identifier newtypes**: `TaskId`, `RunId`, `ToolId`, `PredicateId` (each opaque `String`).
    18	2. **Read/Write set keys**: `ReadKey(String)`, `WriteKey(String)`.
    19	3. **Agent signature**: `AgentSignature([u8; 64])` — Ed25519 detached signature, distinct from `SystemSignature` (system_keypair.rs).
    20	4. **Predicate result types**: `BoolWithProof`, `PredicateResultsBundle`, `SafetyOrCreation`.
    21	5. **Status / class enums**: `TxStatus`, `RejectionClass`, `VerifyVerdict`, `RunOutcome`.
    22	6. **Slash evidence reference**: `SlashEvidenceCid(Cid)` newtype.
    23	7. **Money newtype**: `StakeMicroCoin(MicroCoin)` (non-negative invariant enforced at business layer; type-level newtype prevents accidental mix with general `MicroCoin`).
    24	8. **Typed-tx payload structs**: `WorkTx`, `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`, `ReuseTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`, `TaskExpireTx`. (`TerminalSummaryTx` already exists in `system_keypair.rs`.)
    25	9. **Outer enum**: `pub enum TypedTx` with the 7 variants.
    26	10. **Trait**: `pub trait HasSubmitter` per STATE spec § 3.6.5 v1.3.
    27	11. **Conformance tests**: 1 golden fixture per main tx kind (input → known SHA-256 of canonical bytes) + 100-input round-trip + cross-call byte stability.
    28	
    29	### Out of scope (explicit deferral)
    30	
    31	- **MetaTx + ancillaries** (`PredicatePatch`, `ToolPatch`, `JudgeSignature`, `HumanSignature`, `ConstitutionCheckProof`, `ReversibilityPlan`) — STATE spec § 1.6 declares MetaTx is **v4.1 only**; v4 emits `MetaProposalDraft` to L3 CAS, not L4. ⏭ deferred.
    32	- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
    33	- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
    34	- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
    35	- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
    36	- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
    37	
    38	### What this atom is NOT replacing
    39	
    40	- `src/state/q_state.rs` (existing): keeps its existing types verbatim. CO1.1.4-pre1 only adds new types in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
    41	- `src/economy/money.rs` (existing): unchanged. `StakeMicroCoin` is a **newtype on `MicroCoin`** living in `src/economy/money.rs` (additive).
    42	
    43	---
    44	
    45	## § 1 Module layout
    46	
    47	```
    48	src/state/
    49	├── mod.rs                       (existing; +pub mod typed_tx + re-exports)
    50	├── q_state.rs                   (existing; unchanged)
    51	└── typed_tx.rs                  (NEW; ~600-900 LoC; the ABI surface)
    52	
    53	src/economy/
    54	└── money.rs                     (existing; +pub struct StakeMicroCoin newtype + minimal impls)
    55	
    56	src/bottom_white/ledger/
    57	└── system_keypair.rs            (existing; serde_bytes_64 helper promoted to pub(crate)
    58	                                  so AgentSignature can re-use the [u8; 64] adapter)
    59	```
    60	
    61	**Crate boundary**: `state::typed_tx` consumes (a) `state::q_state` types (Hash, AgentId, TxId, NodeId), (b) `economy::money::MicroCoin` + `StakeMicroCoin`, (c) `bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid`, (d) `bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{SystemEpoch, SystemSignature}`. No new outward dependencies; no circular dep risk.
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	## § 2 Identifier newtypes
    66	
    67	```rust
    68	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    69	pub struct TaskId(pub String);
    70	
    71	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    72	pub struct RunId(pub String);
    73	
    74	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    75	pub struct ToolId(pub String);
    76	
    77	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    78	pub struct PredicateId(pub String);
    79	
    80	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    81	pub struct ReadKey(pub String);
    82	
    83	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    84	pub struct WriteKey(pub String);
    85	```
    86	
    87	All identifiers are opaque strings to Q_t (per existing `AgentId` / `TxId` pattern in q_state.rs). Concrete derivation rules (e.g. `TxId::derive(run_id, "terminal")` per STATE § 3.7) live at the call sites, not in the type.
    88	
    89	---
    90	
    91	## § 3 AgentSignature, StakeMicroCoin, SlashEvidenceCid
    92	
    93	```rust
    94	/// Detached Ed25519 signature over a per-tx canonical_digest.
    95	/// Distinct from SystemSignature (system-keypair signatures) at type level —
    96	/// agent-vs-system signature confusion would be a security hazard.
    97	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    98	pub struct AgentSignature(#[serde(with = "system_keypair::serde_bytes_64")] [u8; 64]);
    99	
   100	/// Newtype on MicroCoin for stake fields. Non-negative is a runtime invariant
   101	/// (not a type invariant) per Inv 3.
   102	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   103	#[serde(transparent)]
   104	pub struct StakeMicroCoin(pub MicroCoin);
   105	
   106	/// L3 CAS handle to slash evidence. Kept as a newtype (not a bare Cid) so the
   107	/// FinalizedSlash variant of TxStatus can't accidentally accept arbitrary CIDs.
   108	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   109	#[serde(transparent)]
   110	pub struct SlashEvidenceCid(pub Cid);
   111	```
   112	
   113	---
   114	
   115	## § 4 FinalizeRewardTx — derived schema
   116	
   117	**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
   118	
   119	**v1 derivation** (from § 3.4 call sites + the TaskExpireTx pattern in § 3.6, system-emitted):
   120	
   121	```rust
   122	pub struct FinalizeRewardTx {
   123	    pub tx_id: TxId,                       //  1
   124	    pub claim_id: TxId,                    //  2  identifies the ClaimsIndex entry being finalized
   125	    pub task_id: TaskId,                   //  3
   126	    pub solver: AgentId,                   //  4  reward recipient
   127	    pub reward: MicroCoin,                 //  5  computed by SettlementEngine
   128	    pub parent_state_root: Hash,           //  6  must equal q.state_root_t at submission
   129	    pub epoch: SystemEpoch,                //  7  which keypair signed
   130	    pub timestamp_logical: u64,            //  8  monotonic
   131	    pub system_signature: SystemSignature, //  9  system-emitted, not agent-signed
   132	}
   133	```
   134	
   135	**Audit input**: this is the spec gap most likely to attract a CHALLENGE. Auditors should verify the field set is sufficient for `finalize_reward_transition` § 3.4 stage 3 (unlock + return solver stake + credit reward + finalize claim + debit escrow + pay royalties along `royalty_graph_t`). If a field is missing (e.g. royalty edges to walk), this atom should add it before proceeding to A2.
   136	
   137	**Honest acknowledgement**: this is the only schema in CO1.1.4-pre1 not directly transcribed from STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1.
   138	
   139	---
   140	
   141	## § 5 Other typed tx schemas (transcribed from STATE spec)
   142	
   143	`WorkTx` (§ 1.2 — 12 fields), `VerifyTx` / `ChallengeTx` / `ReuseTx` (§ 1.3), `TaskExpireTx` (§ 3.6 v1.3 schema). Verbatim transcription; minor adjustments documented inline.
   144	
   145	`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
   146	
   147	---
   148	
   149	## § 6 TypedTx enum
   150	
   151	```rust
   152	pub enum TypedTx {
   153	    Work(WorkTx),
   154	    Verify(VerifyTx),
   155	    Challenge(ChallengeTx),
   156	    Reuse(ReuseTx),
   157	    FinalizeReward(FinalizeRewardTx),
   158	    TaskExpire(TaskExpireTx),
   159	    TerminalSummary(TerminalSummaryTx),  // imported from system_keypair
   160	}
   161	
   162	impl TypedTx {
   163	    pub fn tx_kind(&self) -> TxKind {
   164	        match self {
   165	            Self::Work(_)            => TxKind::Work,
   166	            Self::Verify(_)          => TxKind::Verify,
   167	            Self::Challenge(_)       => TxKind::Challenge,
   168	            Self::Reuse(_)           => TxKind::Reuse,
   169	            Self::FinalizeReward(_)  => TxKind::FinalizeReward,
   170	            Self::TaskExpire(_)      => TxKind::TaskExpire,
   171	            Self::TerminalSummary(_) => TxKind::TerminalSummary,
   172	        }
   173	    }
   174	}
   175	```
   176	
   177	The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
   178	
   179	---
   180	
   181	## § 7 Canonical serialization invariants
   182	
   183	`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
   184	
   185	- I-CANON-A: `canonical_encode(typed_tx)` returns deterministic bytes (BE + fixed_int + BTreeMap lex order).
   186	- I-CANON-B: `decode(encode(x)) == x` byte-identically for ALL variants.
   187	- I-CANON-C: 2 independent encode calls on the same value produce identical bytes.
   188	- I-CANON-D: per-variant golden fixture: 1 hand-crafted instance per tx kind has a known SHA-256 of canonical bytes, hard-coded in tests. Future serde-derive change → fixture diff → audit-required.
   189	
   190	Per STATE spec § 2.5 "Conformance" requirements; § 7 lifts them to invariant status for CO1.1.4-pre1.
   191	
   192	---
   193	
   194	## § 8 HasSubmitter trait
   195	
   196	```rust
   197	pub trait HasSubmitter {
   198	    fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId>;
   199	}
   200	
   201	impl HasSubmitter for WorkTx       { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.agent_id.clone()) } }
   202	impl HasSubmitter for VerifyTx     { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.verifier_agent.clone()) } }
   203	impl HasSubmitter for ChallengeTx  { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.challenger_agent.clone()) } }
   204	impl HasSubmitter for ReuseTx      { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { None } }
   205	// FinalizeRewardTx, TaskExpireTx, TerminalSummaryTx: system-emitted; submitter_id() = None
   206	```
   207	
   208	Implements STATE spec § 3.6.5 v1.3 directive verbatim.
   209	
   210	---
   211	
   212	## § 9 Acknowledged divergences from STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC
   213	
   214	| ID | STATE spec | CO1.1.4-pre1 v1 | Reason |
   215	|---|---|---|---|
   216	| **D-1** | § 1.2 WorkTx field 12 = `status: TxStatus` | **dropped from wire** | TxStatus is runner book-keeping, not canonical wire data. Mixing it forces every encode to make a status decision, conflating wire-format determinism with runtime state machinery. (Audit input.) |
   217	| **D-2** | § 3.4 `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` opaque constructor | **explicit `FinalizeRewardTx` struct** | spec gap; derived schema in § 4 above. |
   218	| **D-3** | § 1.5 `TerminalSummaryTx` | **NOT redefined** here | already shipped in `system_keypair.rs`; CO1.1.4-pre1 imports + reuses; module placement migration (move to typed_tx.rs?) deferred to v1.1 if auditors flag. |
   219	
   220	---
   221	
   222	## § 10 Audit gates
   223	
   224	| Round | Codex | Gemini | Conservative | Action |
   225	|---|---|---|---|---|
   226	| 1 | ⏳ pending | ⏳ pending | TBD | initial v1 audit |
   227	| 2+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
   228	
   229	**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.

   127	### 1.2 WorkTx (12 fields per WP § 5.L4)
   128	
   129	```rust
   130	pub struct WorkTx {
   131	    pub tx_id: TxId,                              //  1
   132	    pub task_id: TaskId,                          //  2  links to TaskMarket entry
   133	    pub parent_state_root: Hash,                  //  3  must equal Q_t.state_root_t at submission
   134	    pub agent_id: AgentId,                        //  4
   135	    pub read_set: BTreeSet<ReadKey>,              //  5  agent MUST declare read deps (DAG attribution)
   136	    pub write_set: BTreeSet<WriteKey>,            //  6  agent MUST declare write targets
   137	    pub proposal_cid: Cid,                        //  7  L3 CAS handle to payload (not raw payload)
   138	    pub predicate_results: PredicateResultsBundle,//  8  filled BY runner, not by agent
   139	    pub stake: StakeMicroCoin,                    //  9  YES_E stake, i64 micro-coin units
   140	    pub signature: AgentSignature,                // 10
   141	    pub timestamp_logical: u64,                   // 11  monotonic counter from runtime, NOT wall clock
   142	    pub status: TxStatus,                         // 12  Pending | Accepted | Rejected(class) | Finalized
   143	}
   144	
   145	pub enum TxStatus {
   146	    Pending,
   147	    Accepted,
   148	    Rejected(RejectionClass),
   149	    FinalizedReward(MicroCoin),
   150	    FinalizedSlash(SlashEvidenceCid),
   151	}
   152	
   153	pub struct PredicateResultsBundle {
   154	    pub acceptance: BTreeMap<PredicateId, BoolWithProof>,
   155	    pub settlement: BTreeMap<PredicateId, BoolWithProof>,
   156	    pub safety_class: SafetyOrCreation,  // determines fail-closed vs fail-open-with-signal
   157	}
   158	```
   159	
   160	### 1.3 VerifyTx, ChallengeTx, ReuseTx (economic § 13)
   161	
   162	```rust
   163	pub struct VerifyTx {
   164	    pub tx_id: TxId,
   165	    pub target_work_tx: TxId,         // the work_tx being verified
   166	    pub verifier_agent: AgentId,
   167	    pub bond: StakeMicroCoin,         // verifier reputation/bond stake
   168	    pub verdict: VerifyVerdict,       // Confirm | Doubt
   169	    pub signature: AgentSignature,
   170	    pub timestamp_logical: u64,
   171	}
   172	
   173	pub struct ChallengeTx {
   174	    pub tx_id: TxId,
   175	    pub target_work_tx: TxId,
   176	    pub challenger_agent: AgentId,
   177	    pub stake: StakeMicroCoin,        // NO_E stake, i64 micro-coin
   178	    pub counterexample_cid: Cid,      // L3 CAS handle to counterexample
   179	    pub signature: AgentSignature,
   180	    pub timestamp_logical: u64,
   181	}
   182	
   183	pub struct ReuseTx {
   184	    pub tx_id: TxId,
   185	    pub reusing_work_tx: TxId,        // the work_tx that triggered the reuse
   186	    pub reused_tool_id: ToolId,       // L2 Tool Registry handle
   187	    pub reused_tool_creator: AgentId, // royalty recipient
   188	    pub timestamp_logical: u64,
   189	}
   190	```
   191	
   192	### 1.4 RejectedAttemptSummary (D-VETO-6 system-stamped, NOT agent self-report)
   193	
   194	```rust
   195	pub struct RejectedAttemptSummary {
   196	    pub failed_attempts_since_last_accept: u32,           // bounded, capped at u32::MAX
   197	    pub failure_class_histogram: BTreeMap<RejectionClass, u32>,  // counts only, no payloads
   198	    pub first_failure_logical_t: Option<u64>,             // for time-to-first-fail signal
   199	    pub last_failure_logical_t: Option<u64>,              // for recency signal
   200	    // NO raw error strings, NO agent payload contents, NO predicate internal traces
   201	}
   202	
   203	pub enum RejectionClass {
   204	    AcceptancePredicateFail(PredicateId),     // public predicates only; private predicates → Opaque
   205	    SettlementPredicateFail(PredicateId),
   206	    StakeInsufficient,
   207	    SignatureInvalid,
   208	    StaleParentRoot,                          // Q_t advanced; agent's view stale
   209	    Opaque,                                   // private predicate failure; classification withheld
   210	    BudgetExceeded,
   211	}
   212	```
   213	
   214	`RejectedAttemptSummary` is stamped **by the white-box predicate runner** onto the next accepted `WorkTx`. Trust boundary: the runner generates this summary; the agent does NOT self-report. Verified at conformance test level.
   215	
   216	### 1.5 TerminalSummaryTx (no-accept run handler)
   217	
   218	```rust
   219	pub struct TerminalSummaryTx {
   220	    pub tx_id: TxId,
   221	    pub task_id: TaskId,
   222	    pub run_id: RunId,
   223	    pub run_outcome: RunOutcome,           // OmegaAccepted | MaxTxExhausted | WallClockCap | ComputeCap | ErrorHalt
   224	    pub total_attempts: u32,
   225	    pub failure_class_histogram: BTreeMap<RejectionClass, u32>,
   226	    pub last_logical_t: u64,
   227	    pub system_signature: SystemSignature,  // signed by runtime keypair, not by any agent
   228	}
   229	```
   230	
   231	If a run terminates without any accepted work_tx, the runtime emits exactly one `TerminalSummaryTx` to L4. This preserves L6 reconstructibility: error class signal is derivable from tape even if no work_tx ever passed.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md | sed -n '291,324p;328,420p;423,476p;478,630p;631,704p;707,764p;787,856p;863,891p'" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
 succeeded in 0ms:
   291	## § 2.5 Canonical Serialization (v1.2 NEW per Codex Q5)
   292	
   293	> **Required because**: `tx.canonical_digest()` is called in spec § 3 stages 2 of WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx, but byte-level format is undefined. STEP_B branch A vs branch B may pick different serialization (JSON sorted keys vs bincode vs Rust derive order) → cross-branch signature verification fails. Mandatory canonical format closes this.
   294	
   295	**Format**: **bincode v2** (`bincode::serde`) with the following constraints:
   296	- **Big-endian byte order** for all multi-byte integers (network order; deterministic across platforms)
   297	- **`BTreeMap` keys serialized in lexicographic byte order** (this is bincode default; verified by test)
   298	- **Strings serialized as UTF-8 with explicit length prefix u32-BE**
   299	- **Optional fields: `0x00` prefix for `None`, `0x01` + value for `Some`**
   300	- **Enum discriminant: u8 (variant index in declaration order)**
   301	- **No padding bytes; no implicit alignment**
   302	
   303	**Application**:
   304	```rust
   305	pub fn canonical_digest<T: Serialize>(value: &T) -> [u8; 32] {
   306	    let bytes = bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(value, bincode_canonical_config()).expect("serialize");
   307	    sha256(&bytes)
   308	}
   309	
   310	fn bincode_canonical_config() -> bincode::config::Configuration {
   311	    bincode::config::standard()
   312	        .with_big_endian()
   313	        .with_fixed_int_encoding()    // no varint; fixed-width for determinism
   314	}
   315	```
   316	
   317	**Conformance**: `tests/canonical_serialization.rs` MUST verify:
   318	- 1 golden tx fixture per tx type (WorkTx / VerifyTx / ChallengeTx / ReuseTx / TerminalSummaryTx); each has known input → known SHA-256 output
   319	- Round-trip: `decode(encode(x)) == x` byte-identical for 100 random inputs
   320	- Stability: 2 independent runs on same input → same bytes
   321	
   322	**STEP_B implication**: branches A and B both use this exact `bincode_canonical_config`; signature verification works cross-branch by construction.
   323	
   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
   328	## § 3 step_transition (Deterministic Pseudocode)
   329	
   330	```rust
   331	/// Pure function. Same (Q_t, tx_i) → byte-identical (Q_{t+1}, signals_t).
   332	/// No I/O. No env reads. No clock reads. No randomness without seed in tx_i.
   333	pub fn step_transition(
   334	    q: &QState,
   335	    tx: &WorkTx,
   336	    registry: &PredicateRegistry,
   337	    tool_registry: &ToolRegistry,
   338	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   339	
   340	    // STAGE 1: parent_state_root match (stale view rejection)
   341	    if tx.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   342	        return Err(TransitionError::StaleParent {
   343	            expected: q.state_root_t,
   344	            got:      tx.parent_state_root,
   345	        });
   346	        // NB: rejection here does NOT change Q_t; runner stamps RejectedAttemptSummary
   347	        // onto the NEXT accepted tx (or onto TerminalSummaryTx if run ends without accept)
   348	    }
   349	
   350	    // STAGE 2: signature verification
   351	    if !verify_signature(&tx.signature, tx.canonical_digest()) {
   352	        return Err(TransitionError::SignatureInvalid);
   353	    }
   354	
   355	    // STAGE 3: stake availability (Inv 5 — YES_E event-bound)
   356	    let agent_balance = q.economic_state_t.balances_t.get(&tx.agent_id);
   357	    if agent_balance < tx.stake {
   358	        return Err(TransitionError::StakeInsufficient { available: agent_balance, required: tx.stake });
   359	    }
   360	
   361	    // STAGE 4: predicate gate (Inv 6 — predicate-gated transition)
   362	    let acceptance_results = registry.run_acceptance(tx, q)?;
   363	    let safety_class = registry.classify(tx);
   364	    match (safety_class, acceptance_results.all_passed()) {
   365	        (SafetyOrCreation::Safety, false) => {
   366	            return Err(TransitionError::AcceptancePredicateFailed(acceptance_results));
   367	            // fail-closed for Safety (WP § 7.2)
   368	        }
   369	        (SafetyOrCreation::Creation, false) => {
   370	            // fail-open-with-signal: still reject, but emit informational signal (no Q_t change)
   371	            return Err(TransitionError::AcceptancePredicateFailed(acceptance_results));
   372	        }
   373	        _ => {}  // passed; continue
   374	    }
   375	
   376	    // STAGE 5: provisional reward issue (Inv 7 — provisional then final)
   377	    let claim = ClaimId::derive(tx.tx_id);
   378	    let provisional_reward = SettlementEngine::issue_provisional(
   379	        claim,
   380	        &q.economic_state_t.escrows_t,
   381	        tx.task_id,
   382	    )?;
   383	
   384	    // STAGE 6: state transition apply (deterministic)
   385	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   386	    q_next.economic_state_t.claims_t.insert(claim, provisional_reward);
   387	    q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.lock(tx.agent_id, tx.task_id, tx.stake);
   388	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.debit(tx.agent_id, tx.stake);
   389	    q_next.q_t.update_per_agent(tx.agent_id, |s| {
   390	        s.last_accepted_tx = Some(tx.tx_id);
   391	        s.retry_counter_for_current_task = 0;  // reset on accept
   392	    });
   393	
   394	    // L4 append
   395	    let new_ledger_root = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, tx);
   396	    q_next.ledger_root_t = new_ledger_root;
   397	
   398	    // L5 materialize
   399	    let new_state_root = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, tx);
   400	    q_next.state_root_t = new_state_root;
   401	
   402	    // L6 signal emit (broadcast price + reputation; NOT evaluator internals — Inv 10)
   403	    let signals = SignalBundle {
   404	        boolean: vec![Signal::Boolean(BoolSignal::AcceptedAt(tx.tx_id))],
   405	        statistical: vec![
   406	            Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::PriceUpdate(price_for(tx.task_id, q_next.economic_state_t.price_index_t))),
   407	            Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(tx.agent_id, +reputation_delta(tx))),
   408	        ],
   409	    };
   410	
   411	    // STAGE 7: head advance
   412	    q_next.head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(new_state_root);
   413	
   414	    // STAGE 8: challenge window open (Inv 7 — finalization is deferred)
   415	    q_next.economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t.open(claim, tx.timestamp_logical, CHALLENGE_WINDOW_TICKS);
   416	
   417	    Ok((q_next, signals))
   418	}
   419	```
   420	
   423	### 3.1 verify_transition (VerifyTx)
   424	
   425	Per Gemini v3.2 review Q10 VETO — extending pseudocode to all state-mutating tx types.
   426	
   427	> **v1.1 note (gap 11.4)**: this pseudocode handles ONE verifier per tx. Multi-verifier quorum aggregation is a TaskMarket config (`verifier_quorum_required: usize` default = 1). When N>1 verifiers each submit verify_tx for the same target_work_tx, claim transitions to `Pending → ApprovedByVerifiers` only after `verifier_quorum_required` distinct verifiers have submitted `Confirm`. Aggregation rule deferred to CO P2.7 atom (Verifier role detail). For v4 default (quorum=1), each verify_tx independently advances claim to ApprovedByVerifiers.
   428	
   429	```rust
   430	pub fn verify_transition(
   431	    q: &QState,
   432	    tx: &VerifyTx,
   433	    registry: &PredicateRegistry,
   434	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   435	
   436	    // STAGE 1: target work_tx must exist + be in Pending or Provisional state
   437	    let target = q.economic_state_t.claims_t.get(&tx.target_work_tx)
   438	        .ok_or(TransitionError::TargetWorkTxNotFound)?;
   439	    if !target.status.allows_verification() {
   440	        return Err(TransitionError::TargetWorkTxNotVerifiable);
   441	    }
   442	
   443	    // STAGE 2: signature + bond
   444	    if !verify_signature(&tx.signature, tx.canonical_digest()) {
   445	        return Err(TransitionError::SignatureInvalid);
   446	    }
   447	    let verifier_balance = q.economic_state_t.balances_t.get(&tx.verifier_agent);
   448	    if verifier_balance < tx.bond {
   449	        return Err(TransitionError::StakeInsufficient);
   450	    }
   451	
   452	    // STAGE 3: predicate gate (verifier predicate, NOT same as work_tx acceptance)
   453	    let verify_results = registry.run_verification(tx, target, q)?;
   454	    if !verify_results.all_passed() {
   455	        return Err(TransitionError::VerificationPredicateFailed(verify_results));
   456	    }
   457	
   458	    // STAGE 4: state transition
   459	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   460	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.debit(tx.verifier_agent, tx.bond);
   461	    q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.lock_verifier_bond(tx.verifier_agent, tx.target_work_tx, tx.bond);
   462	    q_next.economic_state_t.claims_t.add_verification(tx.target_work_tx, tx.verifier_agent, tx.verdict);
   463	
   464	    // STAGE 5: append + materialize + signals
   465	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, tx);
   466	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, tx);
   467	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   468	
   469	    let signals = SignalBundle {
   470	        boolean: vec![Signal::Boolean(BoolSignal::VerifiedAt(tx.tx_id))],
   471	        statistical: vec![Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(tx.verifier_agent, +verify_reputation_delta(tx, target)))],
   472	    };
   473	
   474	    Ok((q_next, signals))
   475	}
   476	```
   478	### 3.2 challenge_transition (ChallengeTx)
   479	
   480	```rust
   481	pub fn challenge_transition(
   482	    q: &QState,
   483	    tx: &ChallengeTx,
   484	    registry: &PredicateRegistry,
   485	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   486	
   487	    // STAGE 1: target work_tx must exist + still in challenge window
   488	    let target = q.economic_state_t.claims_t.get(&tx.target_work_tx)
   489	        .ok_or(TransitionError::TargetWorkTxNotFound)?;
   490	    let window = q.economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t.get(tx.target_work_tx)
   491	        .ok_or(TransitionError::ChallengeWindowClosed)?;
   492	    // v1.4: use ChallengeWindow::is_open(now) per § 5.2.5; same rule used by finalize_reward
   493	    if !window.is_open(tx.timestamp_logical) {
   494	        return Err(TransitionError::ChallengeWindowClosed);
   495	    }
   496	
   497	    // STAGE 2: signature + NO_E stake
   498	    if !verify_signature(&tx.signature, tx.canonical_digest()) {
   499	        return Err(TransitionError::SignatureInvalid);
   500	    }
   501	    let challenger_balance = q.economic_state_t.balances_t.get(&tx.challenger_agent);
   502	    if challenger_balance < tx.stake {
   503	        return Err(TransitionError::StakeInsufficient);
   504	    }
   505	
   506	    // STAGE 3: counterexample acceptance predicate (the BURDEN OF PROOF predicate, Inv 7)
   507	    let counterexample = cas::get(&tx.counterexample_cid)?;
   508	    let counter_check = registry.run_counterexample_check(target, &counterexample, q)?;
   509	    if !counter_check.proves_violation() {
   510	        return Err(TransitionError::CounterexampleInsufficient(counter_check));
   511	    }
   512	
   513	    // STAGE 4: state transition — ROLLBACK target work_tx + slash original solver + reward challenger
   514	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   515	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.debit(tx.challenger_agent, tx.stake);
   516	
   517	    // 4a: rollback target's provisional reward
   518	    let rollback_amount = q.economic_state_t.claims_t.provisional_amount(tx.target_work_tx);
   519	    q_next.economic_state_t.claims_t.mark_slashed(tx.target_work_tx, tx.tx_id);
   520	
   521	    // 4b: slash original solver's stake → reward pool for challenger
   522	    let solver_stake = q.economic_state_t.stakes_t.get(target.solver, target.task_id);
   523	    q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.slash(target.solver, target.task_id);
   524	    q_next.economic_state_t.escrows_t.deposit_from_slash(tx.challenger_agent, solver_stake);
   525	
   526	    // 4c: challenger gets back NO_E stake + slashed solver stake
   527	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(tx.challenger_agent, tx.stake + solver_stake);
   528	
   529	    // 4d: solver reputation -= delta; challenger reputation += delta (Inv 9 immutable but we update via formula not transfer)
   530	    q_next.economic_state_t.reputations_t.adjust(target.solver, -slash_reputation_delta());
   531	    q_next.economic_state_t.reputations_t.adjust(tx.challenger_agent, +challenge_reputation_delta());
   532	
   533	    // 4e: verifier_bond release per task config (gap 11.2 fix; default = return to good-faith verifier)
   534	    //   Rationale: when Carol slashes Alice via challenge, Bob (the verifier) was duped but acted in good faith.
   535	    //   Slashing Bob's bond would discourage future verification. Configurable per TaskMarket.
   536	    //   Applies to ALL verifiers who voted Confirm on the slashed work_tx.
   537	    let bond_release_policy = q.economic_state_t.task_markets_t
   538	        .get(target.task_id)
   539	        .map(|tm| tm.config.verifier_bond_on_slash)
   540	        .unwrap_or(VerifierBondPolicy::ReturnToVerifier);
   541	    for (verifier, bond) in q.economic_state_t.stakes_t.verifier_bonds_for(tx.target_work_tx) {
   542	        match bond_release_policy {
   543	            VerifierBondPolicy::ReturnToVerifier => {
   544	                q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(verifier, bond);
   545	                q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.release_verifier_bond(verifier, tx.target_work_tx);
   546	            }
   547	            VerifierBondPolicy::SlashedToChallenger => {
   548	                q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(tx.challenger_agent, bond);
   549	                q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.slash_verifier_bond(verifier, tx.target_work_tx);
   550	                q_next.economic_state_t.reputations_t.adjust(verifier, -verifier_slash_delta());
   551	            }
   552	        }
   553	    }
   554	
   555	    // STAGE 5: close challenge window
   556	    q_next.economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t.close(tx.target_work_tx, ChallengeOutcome::Slashed(tx.tx_id));
   557	
   558	    // STAGE 6: append + materialize + signals
   559	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, tx);
   560	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, tx);
   561	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   562	
   563	    let signals = SignalBundle {
   564	        boolean: vec![Signal::Boolean(BoolSignal::ChallengeUpheld(tx.tx_id))],
   565	        statistical: vec![
   566	            Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(target.solver, -slash_reputation_delta())),
   567	            Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(tx.challenger_agent, +challenge_reputation_delta())),
   568	        ],
   569	    };
   570	
   571	    Ok((q_next, signals))
   572	}
   573	```
   574	
   575	### 3.3 reuse_transition (ReuseTx)
   576	
   577	```rust
   578	pub fn reuse_transition(
   579	    q: &QState,
   580	    tx: &ReuseTx,
   581	    tool_registry: &ToolRegistry,
   582	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   583	    // STAGE 1: tool must be registered + still active in L2
   584	    let tool = tool_registry.get(tx.reused_tool_id)
   585	        .ok_or(TransitionError::ToolNotInRegistry)?;
   586	    if tool.creator != tx.reused_tool_creator {
   587	        return Err(TransitionError::ToolCreatorMismatch);
   588	    }
   589	
   590	    // STAGE 2: parent reusing_work_tx must exist + Accepted
   591	    let parent = q.economic_state_t.claims_t.get(&tx.reusing_work_tx)
   592	        .ok_or(TransitionError::TargetWorkTxNotFound)?;
   593	    if !parent.status.is_accepted_or_finalized() {
   594	        return Err(TransitionError::ParentNotAcceptedYet);
   595	    }
   596	
   597	    // STAGE 3: state transition — add edge to royalty graph
   598	    //   gap 11.3 fix: weight bounded by MAX_REUSE_ROYALTY_FRACTION = 0.10 default
   599	    //   Rationale: 10% upper bound protects solver's primary reward. Builders earn via creating
   600	    //   widely-reusable tools, not via single high-percentage extractions. Configurable per TaskMarket
   601	    //   for cases where user wants to override (e.g., creator-economy experiments).
   602	    let max_royalty = q.economic_state_t.task_markets_t
   603	        .get(parent.task_id)
   604	        .and_then(|tm| tm.config.max_reuse_royalty_fraction)
   605	        .unwrap_or(MAX_REUSE_ROYALTY_FRACTION_DEFAULT);  // = 0.10 in micro-coin fractional repr (10000 / 100000)
   606	    let bounded_weight = tool.reuse_royalty_share.min(max_royalty);
   607	    if tool.reuse_royalty_share > max_royalty {
   608	        log::warn!(
   609	            "reuse_tx {}: tool {} declared royalty {} > max {}; clamping to {}",
   610	            tx.tx_id, tx.reused_tool_id, tool.reuse_royalty_share, max_royalty, bounded_weight
   611	        );
   612	    }
   613	
   614	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   615	    q_next.economic_state_t.royalty_graph_t.add_edge(
   616	        from: tx.reusing_work_tx,
   617	        to:   tx.reused_tool_id,
   618	        creator: tx.reused_tool_creator,
   619	        weight: bounded_weight,    // clamped per gap 11.3
   620	    );
   621	
   622	    // STAGE 4: append + materialize (no signals; royalty paid at finalize_reward time)
   623	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, tx);
   624	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, tx);
   625	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   626	
   627	    Ok((q_next, SignalBundle::empty()))
   628	}
   629	```
   630	
   631	### 3.4 finalize_reward (challenge window expiry)
   632	
   633	Triggered by tick (no agent submits this; runtime emits when challenge window expires for any provisional claim).
   634	
   635	```rust
   636	pub fn finalize_reward_transition(
   637	    q: &QState,
   638	    claim_id: ClaimId,
   639	    settlement_engine: &SettlementEngine,
   640	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   641	    let claim = q.economic_state_t.claims_t.get(&claim_id)
   642	        .ok_or(TransitionError::ClaimNotFound)?;
   643	    let window = q.economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t.get(claim.target_work_tx);
   644	
   645	    // STAGE 1: window must be expired AND no open slash
   646	    // v1.4: invoke ChallengeWindow::is_open(now) per § 5.2.5 with explicit `now` arg;
   647	    // same rule as challenge_transition stage 1
   648	    if let Some(w) = window {
   649	        if w.is_open(q.q_t.current_round) {
   650	            return Err(TransitionError::ChallengeWindowStillOpen);
   651	        }
   652	        if w.outcome == Some(ChallengeOutcome::Slashed(_)) {
   653	            return Err(TransitionError::AlreadySlashed);  // never finalize a slashed claim
   654	        }
   655	    }
   656	
   657	    // STAGE 2: compute reward per Economic § 21 final formula
   658	    let reward = settlement_engine.finalize(
   659	        claim,
   660	        Escrow::lookup(q, claim.task_id),
   661	        Attribution::lookup(q, claim.target_work_tx),
   662	        Survival::full,  // window expired without slash
   663	        Utility::lookup(q, claim.target_work_tx),
   664	        Constitution::check(q),
   665	    )?;
   666	
   667	    // STAGE 3: state transition
   668	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   669	    let target = claim.target_work_tx_data;
   670	
   671	    // 3a (v1.2 NEW; gap 11.A per Gemini + Codex Q2): unlock + return solver's stake
   672	    // Without this, every successful solver permanently loses their stake → Inv 3 violation.
   673	    let solver_stake_locked = q.economic_state_t.stakes_t.get(target.solver, target.task_id);
   674	    q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.unlock(target.solver, target.task_id);
   675	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(target.solver, solver_stake_locked);
   676	
   677	    // 3b: credit reward + finalize claim + debit escrow
   678	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(target.solver, reward);
   679	    q_next.economic_state_t.claims_t.finalize(claim_id, reward);
   680	    q_next.economic_state_t.escrows_t.debit(claim.task_id, reward);
   681	
   682	    // 3c: pay royalties along royalty_graph_t edges (v1.2 explicit rounding rule per Codex Q3 + Q10)
   683	    // Royalty math uses i64 micro-coin throughout; rounding = integer floor (round-down) to preserve Inv 3.
   684	    // No f64; no implicit casts. weight stored as MicroFraction (i64 in 1_000_000 units representing 0.0..1.0).
   685	    let reward_micro = reward.to_micro_units();    // i64
   686	    for edge in q.economic_state_t.royalty_graph_t.edges_from(claim.target_work_tx) {
   687	        let royalty_micro = reward_micro
   688	            .checked_mul(edge.weight.micro_units())
   689	            .expect("overflow")
   690	            / 1_000_000;    // integer floor; deterministic across platforms
   691	        let royalty = MicroCoin::from_micro_units(royalty_micro);
   692	        q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(edge.creator, royalty);
   693	        q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.debit(target.solver, royalty);  // royalty comes from solver's reward, not extra mint (Inv 4)
   694	    }
   695	    // Note: integer floor means total royalty payments may be < `reward × Σ weights` by up to `n` micro-units (1 per edge);
   696	    // the dust remains in solver's balance. This is intentional and consistent with Bitcoin satoshi rounding.
   697	
   698	    // STAGE 4: emit terminal signals
   699	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, &FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward));
   700	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, &FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward));
   701	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   702	
   703	    Ok((q_next, SignalBundle::finalize(claim_id, reward)))
   704	}
   707	### 3.6 task_expire_transition (v1.3 refactored: pure boundary preserved per Codex re-audit)
   708	
   709	**Why**: a TaskMarket entry has a deadline; if no work_tx is accepted by deadline, the bounty MUST refund to task creator (otherwise Inv 3 monetary conservation broken: bounty trapped in escrow forever).
   710	
   711	**v1.3 fix**: split runtime side effects (signing, logical time assignment) from pure transition. Runtime constructs `TaskExpireTx` BEFORE entering pure transition; pure `task_expire_transition` takes already-signed tx as argument. This restores § 2 + § 3 pure-boundary discipline (Codex Q1.3 + new-issue #1 fix).
   712	
   713	```rust
   714	// PURE transition (used by both branch A and branch B in STEP_B)
   715	pub fn task_expire_transition(
   716	    q: &QState,
   717	    tx: &TaskExpireTx,    // v1.3: already-signed by runtime BEFORE entry
   718	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   719	    let task = q.economic_state_t.task_markets_t.get(tx.task_id)
   720	        .ok_or(TransitionError::TaskNotFound)?;
   721	
   722	    // STAGE 1: signature verification (system signature; not agent)
   723	    if !verify_system_signature(&tx.system_signature, &tx, q.system_pubkey_at_epoch(tx.epoch)) {
   724	        return Err(TransitionError::InvalidSystemSignature);
   725	    }
   726	
   727	    // STAGE 2: parent_state_root match (stale view rejection)
   728	    if tx.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   729	        return Err(TransitionError::StaleParent);
   730	    }
   731	
   732	    // STAGE 3: expiry check — task must be expired AND have NO Pending OR Provisional OR Finalized claim
   733	    // v1.3 fix (Codex new-issue #2): broaden race-protection from "Finalized only" to all claim statuses
   734	    if task.deadline_logical_t > q.q_t.current_round {
   735	        return Err(TransitionError::TaskNotExpired);
   736	    }
   737	    if q.economic_state_t.claims_t.any_claim_for_task(tx.task_id) {
   738	        return Err(TransitionError::TaskHasOpenClaim);    // refund only if NO claim exists at all
   739	    }
   740	
   741	    // STAGE 4: refund bounty from escrow to task creator
   742	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   743	    let bounty = q.economic_state_t.escrows_t.get(tx.task_id);
   744	    q_next.economic_state_t.escrows_t.refund(tx.task_id);
   745	    q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(task.creator, bounty);
   746	
   747	    // STAGE 5: refund any solver stakes still locked on expired task
   748	    for (agent, locked_stake) in q.economic_state_t.stakes_t.all_locked_for_task(tx.task_id) {
   749	        q_next.economic_state_t.stakes_t.unlock(agent, tx.task_id);
   750	        q_next.economic_state_t.balances_t.credit(agent, locked_stake);
   751	    }
   752	
   753	    // STAGE 6: remove task from active markets
   754	    q_next.economic_state_t.task_markets_t.remove(tx.task_id);
   755	
   756	    // STAGE 7: append + materialize + signal (purely on tx, q)
   757	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, tx);
   758	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, tx);
   759	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   760	
   761	    let signals = SignalBundle::task_expired(tx.task_id, bounty);
   762	
   763	    Ok((q_next, signals))
   764	}
   787	### 3.6.5 Agent Implicit Init (v1.3 fixed: trait-based submitter resolution per Codex re-audit Q1.4)
   788	
   789	**Where**: applies to ALL agent-submitted transitions (work_transition / verify_transition / challenge_transition / reuse_transition). Inline at stage 4 of each, before user-state mutations.
   790	
   791	**v1.3 fix**: WorkTx has `agent_id`; VerifyTx has `verifier_agent`; ChallengeTx has `challenger_agent`; ReuseTx has no submitting-agent field (it's a fact-tx). Introduce a `Tx::submitter_id() -> Option<AgentId>` trait method that each tx implements explicitly:
   792	
   793	```rust
   794	pub trait HasSubmitter {
   795	    fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId>;
   796	}
   797	
   798	impl HasSubmitter for WorkTx       { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.agent_id.clone()) } }
   799	impl HasSubmitter for VerifyTx     { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.verifier_agent.clone()) } }
   800	impl HasSubmitter for ChallengeTx  { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { Some(self.challenger_agent.clone()) } }
   801	impl HasSubmitter for ReuseTx      { fn submitter_id(&self) -> Option<AgentId> { None }    // ReuseTx has no submitting agent; reuse facts derive from L4 read_set }
   802	
   803	// In each agent-submitted transition's stage 4, INLINE this snippet:
   804	fn implicit_init_agent_if_new(q_next: &mut QState, tx: &impl HasSubmitter) {
   805	    if let Some(submitter) = tx.submitter_id() {
   806	        if !q_next.q_t.agents.contains_key(&submitter) {
   807	            q_next.q_t.agents.insert(submitter, PerAgentState {
   808	                reputation_snapshot: Reputation::default_initial(),    // = 0
   809	                last_accepted_tx: None,
   810	                retry_counter_for_current_task: 0,
   811	            });
   812	        }
   813	    }
   814	}
   815	```
   816	
   817	**Rule**: each transition function MUST call `implicit_init_agent_if_new(&mut q_next, tx)` as the FIRST statement of stage 4 (after stage 3 predicate gate, before any user-state mutation). For `ReuseTx`, `submitter_id()` returns None; no init happens; that's intentional (ReuseTx has no submitting agent to init).
   818	
   819	**Why implicit (not explicit `register_agent_transition`)**:
   820	- Satoshi parallel: Bitcoin addresses are implicitly created at first use; no separate register step
   821	- Avoids gatekeeping: any agent submitting a valid signed tx joins the system
   822	- v4 single-user friendly: gretjia + Codex/Gemini auto-discoverable
   823	- v4.1+: if needed, can add explicit `agent_register_tx` later WITHOUT breaking implicit-init (new tx is purely additive)
   824	
   825	### 3.7 emit_terminal_summary (run-end without acceptance)
   826	
   827	```rust
   828	pub fn emit_terminal_summary_transition(
   829	    q: &QState,
   830	    run_id: RunId,
   831	    runtime: &Runtime,
   832	) -> Result<(QState, SignalBundle), TransitionError> {
   833	    let run = runtime.run_state(run_id)?;
   834	    if run.has_accepted_work_tx() {
   835	        return Err(TransitionError::TerminalSummaryNotApplicable);  // only emitted for no-accept runs
   836	    }
   837	
   838	    let summary = TerminalSummaryTx {
   839	        tx_id: TxId::derive(run_id, "terminal"),
   840	        task_id: run.task_id,
   841	        run_id,
   842	        run_outcome: run.outcome(),
   843	        total_attempts: run.attempt_counter(),
   844	        failure_class_histogram: run.failure_histogram(),
   845	        last_logical_t: run.last_logical_t(),
   846	        system_signature: runtime.system_keypair().sign(canonical_digest_terminal(run)),
   847	    };
   848	
   849	    // STAGE: append; materialize; emit failure-class signals to L6
   850	    let mut q_next = q.clone();
   851	    q_next.ledger_root_t = ledger::append(&q.ledger_root_t, &summary);
   852	    q_next.state_root_t  = materializer::apply(&q.state_root_t, &summary);
   853	    q_next.head_t        = NodeId::from_state_root(q_next.state_root_t);
   854	
   855	    let signals = SignalBundle::terminal_summary(&summary);
   856	
   863	## § 4 Named Invariants (machine-checkable)
   864	
   865	| ID | Invariant | Enforced at | Conformance test |
   866	|---|---|---|---|
   867	| I-DET | Same (Q_t, tx) → byte-identical (Q_{t+1}, signals) | step_transition stage 6-8 | `tests/transition_determinism.rs` |
   868	| I-DETHASH | `state_root_t` after replay from genesis matches authoritative state | replay test | `tests/q_state_reconstruct.rs` |
   869	| I-NOSIDE | step_transition reads only (q, tx, registries); no I/O | static analysis grep + cargo-deny | `tests/no_hidden_inputs.rs` |
   870	| I-PARENT | tx.parent_state_root must equal q.state_root_t | stage 1 | `tests/stale_parent_rejection.rs` |
   871	| I-SIG | tx.signature verifies against tx.canonical_digest() | stage 2 | `tests/signature_verification.rs` |
   872	| I-STAKE | tx.stake ≤ q.balances_t[tx.agent_id]; debit atomic | stage 3, 6 | `tests/stake_atomicity.rs` |
   873	| I-PRED-GATE | rejected work_tx does NOT advance state_root_t | stage 4 | `tests/economic_invariant_INV6_predicate_gated.rs` |
   874	| I-PROV | accepted work_tx → provisional claim, NOT finalized reward | stage 5 | `tests/economic_invariant_INV7_provisional_then_final.rs` |
   875	| I-LOGTIME | timestamp_logical strictly monotonic per-tx; no wall clock | stage 6 | `tests/no_wall_clock_in_tx.rs` |
   876	| I-MICROCOIN | all monetary fields are MicroCoin (i64 newtype) | type system | compile-time + `tests/no_f64_money.rs` |
   877	| I-BTREE | Q_t indices use BTreeMap, not HashMap (deterministic order) | type system | `tests/q_state_uses_btree.rs` |
   878	| I-NOSIDECAR | no Vec/HashMap "graveyard"-like sidecar (Art. 0.2) | static analysis | `tests/no_rejection_sidecar.rs` |
   879	| I-RETRY | RejectedAttemptSummary stamped by runner, not agent | stamp call site | `tests/retry_summary_runner_signed.rs` |
   880	| I-TERMINAL | every run terminates with at least one of: accepted work_tx OR TerminalSummaryTx | run finalize hook | `tests/run_terminal_invariant.rs` |
   881	| I-NOENV | step_transition dependency tree contains no `std::env` access | cargo-deny + grep | `tests/no_env_in_transition.rs` |
   882	| I-FREEZE-CONFIG | TAPE_ECONOMY_V2 + FOUNDER_GRANT_GAMMA + system_lp_amount frozen at task creation, not at tx submission | TaskMarket::publish | `tests/task_config_frozen_at_publish.rs` |
   883	| **I-NORANDOM** (added per Gemini v3.2 review Q1) | Any tx that consumes randomness MUST seed PRNG from `(tx.tx_id, q.state_root_t)`; no system entropy in step_transition path | step_transition stages 1-7 | `tests/no_runtime_entropy.rs` |
   884	| **I-VERIFY-LIVE** (added per Gemini v3.2 review Q10) | VerifyTx targets MUST be in Pending or Provisional state; cannot verify Accepted-and-finalized or Slashed | verify_transition stage 1 | `tests/verify_target_liveness.rs` |
   885	| **I-CHAL-WINDOW** (added per Gemini v3.2 review Q10) | ChallengeTx must be received within target's challenge_cases_t window; no challenges after window close | challenge_transition stage 1 | `tests/challenge_window_enforced.rs` |
   886	| **I-FINALIZE-EXCLUSIVE** (added) | FinalizeRewardTx and SlashTx are mutually exclusive per claim_id; system runtime serializes | finalize_reward_transition stage 2 | `tests/finalize_or_slash_exclusive.rs` |
   887	| **I-VBOND-RELEASE** (v1.1, gap 11.2 fix) | Verifier bond release on slashed work_tx follows TaskMarket.config.verifier_bond_on_slash policy; default = `ReturnToVerifier`; verifier reputation NOT adjusted under default policy | challenge_transition stage 4e | `tests/verifier_bond_release.rs` |
   888	| **I-ROYALTY-CAP** (v1.1, gap 11.3 fix) | reuse_tx edge weight ≤ TaskMarket.config.max_reuse_royalty_fraction (default 0.10); excess clamped + warning logged | reuse_transition stage 3 | `tests/royalty_cap_enforced.rs` |
   889	| **I-STAKE-RETURN** (v1.2 NEW per Gemini Q2 + Codex Q2) | Successful unchallenged finalize_reward returns + unlocks solver's locked stake exactly once (in addition to reward credit). Test attempts double-claim. | finalize_reward_transition stage 3a | `tests/stake_return_on_finalize.rs` |
   890	| **I-BOUNTY-REFUND** (v1.2 NEW per Gemini Q2 + Codex Q2) | task_expire_transition refunds full bounty to creator + refunds any locked solver stakes when no claim finalized by deadline | task_expire_transition stages 2-3 | `tests/bounty_refund_on_expire.rs` |
   891	| **I-FINALIZE-BATCH-ORDER** (v1.3 corrected: single key throughout) | When N claims become finalizable at the same logical_t, finalize_tx emit order is `(expires_at_logical ASC, claim_id ASC)` — `claim_id` (NOT `target_work_tx`) used everywhere: invariant + § 5.2.3 + conformance test all consistent. | runtime finalize loop + § 5.2.3 | `tests/finalize_batch_order.rs` |

   899	## § 5 Optional TLA+ Skeleton (deferred to spec-gate audit)
   900	
   971	### 5.2.3 Finalize Batch Order
   972	
   973	When N claims expire at the same `logical_t`:
   974	- Order = `(claim.expires_at_logical ASC, claim.claim_id ASC)` (stable, deterministic) — v1.3 fix: uses `claim_id` consistently (NOT `target_work_tx`) to align with `I-FINALIZE-BATCH-ORDER` invariant + conformance test
   975	- Sequencer emits `finalize_reward_transition` ONE AT A TIME in this order
   976	- Each finalize advances state_root before next finalize starts
   977	
   978	### 5.2.4 Conformance Tests
   979	
   980	- `tests/l4_sequencer_serialization.rs` — concurrent submit; assert single-threaded execution by sequencer; same input order → same state_root
   981	- `tests/cross_cell_isolation.rs` — 5 cells run; assert disjoint state_roots; no cross-contamination
   982	- `tests/finalize_batch_order.rs` — 3 claims expire same tick; assert ordering by (expires_at, claim_id); 2 runs byte-identical
   983

   989	    /// Both challenge_transition stage 1 AND finalize_reward stage 1 MUST invoke this method
   990	    /// (NOT hand-code the inequality) to guarantee consistent edge semantics.
   991	    pub fn is_open(&self, now: u64) -> bool {

   997	**Invariant binding**: `I-CHALLENGE-WINDOW-EDGE` enforces that BOTH transition functions call `is_open(now)` rather than hand-coding the boundary check.      2	//!
     3	//! TRACE_MATRIX Art 0.1 — 四要素映射: `QState` provides the tape/control mapping.

     6	//! TRACE_MATRIX WP § 0 axiom 1 — state monotonicity: Q_t evolves only via accepted transitions.

    11	//! Codex flagged `kernel.rs:187-204` HashMap nondeterminism in round-2).

    13	//! Sub-types whose entry shapes are scoped to later atoms (CO P2.x economic engine,
    14	//! CO1.7 transition_ledger) are intentionally minimal here — full schemas land per atom,

    27	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 1.1 — generic 32-byte hash (sha256). State / ledger / registry roots.
    28	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    29	pub struct Hash(pub [u8; 32]);
    30	
    31	impl Hash {
    32	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 1.1 — additive identity (genesis state-root, ledger-root, etc.).
    33	    pub const ZERO: Hash = Hash([0u8; 32]);
    34	
    35	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 1.1 — construct from a 32-byte digest (sha256 output).
    36	    pub fn from_bytes(b: [u8; 32]) -> Self {
    37	        Hash(b)
    38	    }
    39	}
    40	
    41	impl Default for Hash {
    42	    fn default() -> Self {
    43	        Hash::ZERO
    44	    }
    45	}
    46	
    47	/// TRACE_MATRIX Art 0.4 — `head_t` = git commit SHA in Path B substrate (40 hex chars).
    48	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
    49	pub struct NodeId(pub String);
    50	
    51	impl NodeId {
    52	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 — pseudocode `NodeId::from_state_root(state_root)` constructor.
    53	    /// Concrete derivation (commit-tree-of-state-root) lands in CO1.7 transition_ledger.
    54	    pub fn from_state_root(state_root: Hash) -> Self {
    55	        let mut s = String::with_capacity(64);
    56	        for byte in state_root.0.iter() {
    57	            s.push_str(&format!("{:02x}", byte));
    58	        }
    59	        NodeId(s)
    60	    }
    61	}

   298	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   299	// QState — § 1.1 verbatim, 9 fields.
   300	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   301	
   302	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 1.1 — system state Q_t. 9 fields per WP § 4 + economic § 2 amendment.
   303	///
   304	/// Reconstructibility: every field is derivable from L4 transition ledger replay
   305	/// (Art IV Boot 公理).
   306	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   307	pub struct QState {
   308	    /// Agent swarm sub-state (tape head per agent + per-agent reputation snapshots).
   309	    pub q_t: AgentSwarmState,
   310	    /// Current ChainTape head pointer = git commit SHA in Path B substrate.
   311	    pub head_t: NodeId,
   312	    /// Materialized state Merkle root (git tree root in Path B).
   313	    pub state_root_t: Hash,
   314	    /// Agent-visible projection of tape filtered by per-agent visibility policy.
   315	    pub tape_view_t: AgentVisibleProjection,
   316	    /// L4 Transition Ledger root (Merkle root of all accepted tx so far).
   317	    pub ledger_root_t: Hash,
   318	    /// L1 Predicate Registry root.
   319	    pub predicate_registry_root_t: Hash,
   320	    /// L2 Tool Registry root.
   321	    pub tool_registry_root_t: Hash,
   322	    /// Economic state (WP § 2 amendment, 9 sub-fields).
   323	    pub economic_state_t: EconomicState,
   324	    /// Global budget snapshot.
   325	    pub budget_state_t: BudgetSnapshot,
   326	}
   327	
   328	impl QState {
   329	    /// TRACE_MATRIX Art IV Boot — genesis Q_t. All zero / empty;
   330	    /// roots populated by `boot::verify_trust_root` and the `state_root_t` published
   331	    /// in `genesis_payload.toml [constitution_root]`.
   332	    pub fn genesis() -> Self {
   333	        QState::default()
   334	    }
   335	}
   336	
   337	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   338	// Inline determinism tests (round-trip + insertion-order independence).
   339	// Conformance tests proper live in tests/{four_element_mapping, q_state_reconstruct,
   340	// economic_state_reconstruct, six_axioms_alignment}.rs per TRACE_MATRIX_v3.
   341	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   342	
   343	#[cfg(test)]
   344	mod tests {
   345	    use super::*;
   346	
   347	    #[test]
   348	    fn genesis_q_state_is_total_and_default() {
   349	        let g = QState::genesis();
   350	        assert_eq!(g, QState::default());
   351	        assert_eq!(g.q_t.current_round, 0);
   352	        assert!(g.q_t.agents.is_empty());
   353	        assert_eq!(g.head_t, NodeId::default());
   354	        assert_eq!(g.state_root_t, Hash::ZERO);
   355	    }
   356	
   357	    #[test]
   358	    fn nine_field_count_via_serde_json() {
   359	        // Sanity that QState has exactly 9 top-level fields.
   360	        let s = serde_json::to_value(QState::genesis()).unwrap();
   361	        let obj = s.as_object().expect("object");
   362	        assert_eq!(
   363	            obj.len(),
   364	            9,
   365	            "QState must have exactly 9 fields per WP § 4; got {}",
   366	            obj.len()
   367	        );
   368	        for k in &[
   369	            "q_t",
   370	            "head_t",
   371	            "state_root_t",
   372	            "tape_view_t",
   373	            "ledger_root_t",
   374	            "predicate_registry_root_t",
   375	            "tool_registry_root_t",
   376	            "economic_state_t",
   377	            "budget_state_t",
   378	        ] {
   379	            assert!(obj.contains_key(*k), "QState missing field {}", k);
   380	        }
   381	    }
   382	
   383	    #[test]
   384	    fn economic_state_has_nine_sub_fields() {
   385	        let e = EconomicState::default();
   386	        let s = serde_json::to_value(&e).unwrap();
   387	        let obj = s.as_object().unwrap();
   388	        assert_eq!(
   389	            obj.len(),
   390	            9,
   391	            "EconomicState must have 9 sub-fields per WP § 2; got {}",
   392	            obj.len()
   393	        );
   394	    }
   395	
   396	    #[test]
   397	    fn btreemap_insertion_order_independent_serialization() {
   398	        // Insertion-order independence (Inv determinism).
   399	        let mut a = BalancesIndex::default();
   400	        a.0.insert(AgentId("alice".into()), MicroCoin::from_coin(10).unwrap());
   401	        a.0.insert(AgentId("bob".into()), MicroCoin::from_coin(20).unwrap());
   402	
   403	        let mut b = BalancesIndex::default();
   404	        b.0.insert(AgentId("bob".into()), MicroCoin::from_coin(20).unwrap());
   405	        b.0.insert(AgentId("alice".into()), MicroCoin::from_coin(10).unwrap());
   406	
   407	        let sa = serde_json::to_string(&a).unwrap();
   408	        let sb = serde_json::to_string(&b).unwrap();
   409	        assert_eq!(sa, sb, "BTreeMap must yield identical bytes regardless of insertion order");
   410	    }
   411	
   412	    #[test]
   413	    fn node_id_from_state_root_is_deterministic() {
   414	        let r = Hash::from_bytes([0xAB; 32]);
   415	        let n1 = NodeId::from_state_root(r);
   416	        let n2 = NodeId::from_state_root(r);
   417	        assert_eq!(n1, n2);
   418	        assert_eq!(n1.0.len(), 64, "40-byte git SHA hex form would be 40; we use full 32-byte sha256 hex = 64");
   419	    }
   420	}

A few mismatches are already visible: the state spec requires an 8-field `TerminalSummaryTx`, while the code imports a 3-field private-placeholder struct; the typed-tx tests also do not cover `TypedTx::TerminalSummary`. I’m now checking bincode’s actual encoding behavior and running the local target tests to separate design issues from codec myths.

# Codex CO1.1.4-pre1 Round-1 Audit

## Q-A D-1 TxStatus elision
**PASS with spec patch note.** Dropping `WorkTx.status` is sound for accepted-transition replay: STATE `step_transition` does not read `tx.status`; it derives claim/agent state from accepted tx history. The status reads in §3 are from Q/ClaimsIndex, not from received `WorkTx` ([STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:436), [STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:590)). Code elides it at [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:199). Migration if wrong: add it now before ABI freeze, but better is separate runtime/claim status, not a mutable signed WorkTx field.

## Q-B D-2 FinalizeRewardTx derivation
**CHALLENGE.** Royalty edges should be recomputed from Q_t, not put on wire: `royalty_graph_t.edges_from(...)` is already Q-derived in STATE §3.4 ([STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:682)). But the schema should not use bare `TxId` for `claim_id`; STATE speaks in `ClaimId` and finalization ordering is by `claim_id` ([STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:891)). Add a `ClaimId` newtype now. Also specify that `task_id`, `solver`, and `reward` must be verified against Q/SettlementEngine, since code currently stores them as signed assertions ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:266)).

## Q-C D-3 TerminalSummaryTx divergence
**CHALLENGE, must fix now.** STATE requires 8 fields ([STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:219)); shipped code imports a private 3-field placeholder ([system_keypair.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:186)). This is exactly the ABI surface being frozen. Future additions are fixture-breaking and likely decode-breaking. Move/own the full tx schema in `state::typed_tx`; make `system_keypair` sign an opaque digest to avoid `bottom_white -> state` dependency leakage.

## Q-D Bincode settings & cross-platform stability
**CHALLENGE on spec/code parity.** BTreeMap order is deterministic because Rust BTreeMap iteration is sorted and bincode serializes the map in provided iteration order. But STATE §2.5 is wrong for the actual codec: bincode serde encodes enum variant indices as `u32` ([bincode ser](/home/zephryj/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/bincode-2.0.1/src/features/serde/ser.rs:186)), fixed BE emits 4 bytes ([bincode impls](/home/zephryj/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/bincode-2.0.1/src/enc/impls.rs:68)); lengths are `usize`, fixed as u64 BE ([bincode impls](/home/zephryj/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/bincode-2.0.1/src/enc/impls.rs:128)), not u32. `#[repr(u8)]` does not control serde enum wire format. `#[serde(transparent)]` newtypes are wire-identical to their inner values.

## Q-E AgentSignature/SystemSignature distinction
**CHALLENGE.** Type distinction is useful but not sufficient as a security boundary. `AgentSignature` reuses the same 64-byte adapter ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:68)); `SystemSignature` is domain-separated through `CanonicalMessage` ([system_keypair.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:470)). Agent tx signing has no comparable signed-payload/domain spec, and comments imply an impossible “digest excluding signature” without a signing payload ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:194)). Add explicit agent signing payloads/domain prefixes.

## Q-F SignalBundle minimal v1
**CHALLENGE.** It is enough to compile stubs, not enough for CO1.7.5 bodies. STATE emits accepted/verified/challenge signals with boolean/statistical content ([STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:403), [STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:469)); code only has Empty/Finalize/TaskExpired/TerminalSummary ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:461)). TerminalSummary also lacks failure histogram, which STATE says preserves L6 reconstructibility.

## Q-G TransitionError taxonomy
**CHALLENGE.** The enum does not cover “every variant invoked in §3 pseudocode” despite the spec claim. Missing: `SignatureInvalid`, `StakeInsufficient`, predicate failures, target not found/not verifiable, challenge window closed, counterexample insufficient, tool errors, parent not accepted, etc. Compare STATE errors at [STATE spec](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:350) and code at [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:402). `NotYetImplemented` is acceptable only as a temporary stub sentinel, not a stable production error.

## Q-H HasSubmitter correctness
**PASS.** Work/Verify/Challenge delegate to the actual submitter; Reuse returns `None`, correctly treating `reused_tool_creator` as royalty recipient, not submitter ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:337)). Outer delegation is correct ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:379)).

## Q-I Atom scope creep
**PASS with caveat.** `Default`/serde additions are additive. `serde_bytes_64 pub(crate)` is reasonable. The caveat is TerminalSummary placement: keeping typed transaction schema in `system_keypair.rs` leaks tx ABI ownership into the signer module and blocks the full schema cleanly.

## Q-J New defects
Tests pass (`cargo test --lib`: 215/0), but the tests do not enforce the ABI. “Golden” tests only check 64-char length/self-stability, not hardcoded hex ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:742)). `TypedTx::TerminalSummary` is not included in round-trip/kind/golden tests ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:645)). `RejectedAttemptSummary` is still the old private string/hash system-keypair type, not STATE §1.4’s histogram schema ([system_keypair.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:153)). There are also future type mismatches: tx schemas use `TaskId`, while current QState task/escrow/stake indices are still keyed by `TxId`.

## **VERDICT**: CHALLENGE

## Top 3 must-fix (if CHALLENGE)
1. Replace `TerminalSummaryTx` with the full 8-field STATE schema and add TerminalSummary tests/fixtures.
2. Fix canonical serialization documentation/tests: hardcode golden hex, include all variants, and document actual bincode u32 enum/u64 length behavior.
3. Add `ClaimId`, complete `TransitionError`, and define signature-domain signing payloads excluding signature fields.

## Conviction (low/med/high)
**High.** The defects are concrete ABI/spec parity issues, but they are patchable; no VETO-level foundational flaw found.
